Experiment_2_compare_controls

Dominic Rebindaine

2025-09-22

Load required packages

require(tidyverse)
require(lubridate)
require(data.table)
require(tm)
require(parameters)
require(cropgrowdays)

Configure input/output

data.dir = '/Users/dominic/Library/Mobile Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/Documents/PhD/development_2022_2023/analysis/experiment_2/Data'

output.dir = '/Users/dominic/Library/Mobile Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/Documents/PhD/development_2022_2023/analysis/experiment_2/figures'

Load and restructure bud data

#####################
# Load bud set data #
#####################


bud.df = read.table(paste(data.dir,"bud_measurements_rm_2022.csv",sep="/"), header=T, sep=",") %>% 
  
  #create ID and treatment columns
  mutate(ID=readr::parse_number(TreeID), #keep only numbers in string
         TreatmentLetter=removeNumbers(TreeID),#remove numbers in string
         date = as.Date(date, format="%d.%m.%Y"),
         #merge controls
         Treatment = ifelse(TreatmentLetter %in% c("A","C"),"Control",
                            ifelse(TreatmentLetter %in% c("D"),"Pre_chamber_control",
                              ifelse(TreatmentLetter %in% c("E"),"Pre_day", 
                                ifelse(TreatmentLetter %in% c("F"),"Pre_night",
                                   ifelse(TreatmentLetter %in% c("G"),"Pre_full", 
                                       ifelse(TreatmentLetter %in% c("H"),"Post_chamber_control",
                                          ifelse(TreatmentLetter %in% c("I"),"Post_day",
                                              ifelse(TreatmentLetter %in% c("J"),"Post_night",
                                                 ifelse(TreatmentLetter %in% c("K"), "Post_full", "Other"))))))))))
  
  #long format
  bud.df = pivot_longer(bud.df, -c(TreeID, ID, Treatment, TreatmentLetter, week, doy, date), 
               names_to = "bud_type", values_to = "bud_length") %>%
    # remove .length from bud type
  mutate(bud_type = gsub(".length","", bud_type)) %>% 
    # remove B treatment, and NAs
  filter(!Treatment %in% c('Other'),
         !is.na(bud_length)) %>% 
    # group to individ bud
  group_by(ID, bud_type) %>% 
   # only keep buds with more than 8 measurements
  filter(n() >= 8) %>% 
  ungroup()

Sample size

# Observed buds per treatment
table(bud.df[bud.df$date=="2022-09-01",]$Treatment, bud.df[bud.df$date=="2022-09-01",]$bud_type)
##                       
##                        apical lateral
##   Control                  36      36
##   Post_chamber_control     16      18
##   Post_day                 15      18
##   Post_full                17      17
##   Post_night               18      18
##   Pre_chamber_control      16      18
##   Pre_day                  17      17
##   Pre_full                 17      18
##   Pre_night                17      18
# Number of individual trees
length(unique(bud.df$ID))
## [1] 180
# Number of individual trees per treatment
length(unique(bud.df[bud.df$Treatment=="Control",]$ID))
## [1] 36
length(unique(bud.df[bud.df$Treatment=="Pre_chamber_control",]$ID))
## [1] 18
length(unique(bud.df[bud.df$Treatment=="Pre_day",]$ID))
## [1] 18
length(unique(bud.df[bud.df$Treatment=="Pre_night",]$ID))
## [1] 18
length(unique(bud.df[bud.df$Treatment=="Pre_full",]$ID))
## [1] 18
length(unique(bud.df[bud.df$Treatment=="Post_chamber_control",]$ID))
## [1] 18
length(unique(bud.df[bud.df$Treatment=="Post_day",]$ID))
## [1] 18
length(unique(bud.df[bud.df$Treatment=="Post_night",]$ID))
## [1] 18
length(unique(bud.df[bud.df$Treatment=="Post_full",]$ID))
## [1] 18

Derive EOS

####################################################
## Get absolute and relative bud growth
####################################################


bud.df = bud.df %>%
  group_by(ID, bud_type)%>%
  mutate(
    #get relative bud length per individual
    bud.rel = bud_length / max(bud_length),
    #get bud growth rates per individual
    across(bud_length, ~c(NA, diff(.)), .names = "bud_growth"),
    #get relative bud growth rates per individual
    across(bud.rel, ~c(NA, diff(.)), .names = "relbud_growth"),
    #get date of maximum bud length per individual
    date.max.bud = date[which.max(bud_length)]) %>%
  ungroup()


######################################################################
## Mean abs and rel bud growth per treatment
######################################################################

minbud.df = bud.df %>% 
  group_by(ID, bud_type) %>% 
  filter(row_number() <= which.min(bud_length)) %>% 
  select(ID, Treatment, bud_type, bud_length, bud.rel, date.max.bud)

maxbud.df = bud.df %>% 
  group_by(ID, bud_type) %>% 
  filter(row_number() == min(which(bud.rel > 0.9))) %>% 
  select(ID, Treatment, bud_type, bud_length, bud.rel, date.max.bud)

minmaxbud.df = merge(minbud.df, maxbud.df, by = c('ID', 'bud_type'))

minmaxbud.df = minmaxbud.df %>% 
  group_by(ID, bud_type) %>% 
  mutate(diff = bud_length.y - bud_length.x,
         total.rel.growth = (1-bud_length.x/bud_length.y)*100)

averages = minmaxbud.df %>% 
  group_by(Treatment.x) %>% 
  summarise(meandiff = round(mean(diff),2),
            mean.total.rel = round(mean(total.rel.growth),2))


######################################################################
## Get buds that are >= 90% their total length at first measurement
######################################################################

over90start = bud.df %>% filter(doy == 237) %>% filter(bud.rel >= 0.9)


######################################################################
## Interpolate 90% bud set dates from seasonal bud length measurements
######################################################################


bud.data.90 = bud.df %>%
  group_by(ID, Treatment, bud_type) %>%
  # remove buds that were >= 90% relative bud length on day 1
  filter(!(ID %in% c(116) & bud_type == 'apical'),
         !(ID %in% c(148) & bud_type == 'apical'),
         !(ID %in% c(149) & bud_type == 'apical'),
         !(ID %in% c(156) & bud_type == 'apical'),
         !(ID %in% c(158) & bud_type == 'apical'),
         !(ID %in% c(58) & bud_type == 'lateral'),
         !(ID %in% c(80) & bud_type == 'lateral'),
         !(ID %in% c(82) & bud_type == 'lateral'),
         !(ID %in% c(99) & bud_type == 'lateral'),
         !(ID %in% c(147) & bud_type == 'lateral'),
         !(ID %in% c(153) & bud_type == 'lateral'),
         !(ID %in% c(154) & bud_type == 'lateral'),
         !(ID %in% c(156) & bud_type == 'lateral'),
         !(ID %in% c(157) & bud_type == 'lateral'),
         !(ID %in% c(158) & bud_type == 'lateral'),
         !(ID %in% c(159) & bud_type == 'lateral')) %>% 
  # Find the last row with <90% bud length in each group, keep only this row and next
   filter(row_number() <= min(which(bud.rel > 0.9)),
          row_number() >= min(which(bud.rel > 0.9))-1) %>%
  #Linear interpolation
  summarize(EOS     = as.Date(approx(bud.rel, date, .9,  ties=min)$y, origin="1970-01-01")) %>%
  mutate(EOS.DOY    = yday(EOS)) %>%
  ungroup()
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'ID', 'Treatment'. You can override using
## the `.groups` argument.
# Observed buds per treatment after removal
table(bud.data.90$Treatment, bud.data.90$bud_type)
##                       
##                        apical lateral
##   Control                  36      36
##   Post_chamber_control     16      18
##   Post_day                 11      11
##   Post_full                17      17
##   Post_night               18      18
##   Pre_chamber_control      16      17
##   Pre_day                  17      15
##   Pre_full                 16      18
##   Pre_night                17      17
# Buds analysis data summary
##############################
BudsSummary = merge(bud.data.90, minmaxbud.df, by = c('ID', 'bud_type')) %>% 
  select(ID, bud_type, Treatment, EOS, EOS.DOY, bud_length.x, bud_length.y, diff, total.rel.growth)

AverageSummary = BudsSummary %>% 
  group_by(Treatment) %>% 
  summarise(
    meanEOS.DOY = round(mean(EOS.DOY),1),
            ci.lower.EOS = round(meanEOS.DOY - 1.96*standard_error(EOS.DOY),1),
            ci.upper.EOS = round(meanEOS.DOY + 1.96*standard_error(EOS.DOY),1),
    meandiff = round(mean(diff),2),
            ci.lower.diff = round(meandiff - 1.96*standard_error(diff),2),
            ci.upper.diff = round(meandiff + 1.96*standard_error(diff),2),
    mean.total.rel = round(mean(total.rel.growth),2),
            ci.lower.rel = round(mean.total.rel - 1.96*standard_error(total.rel.growth),2),
            ci.upper.rel = round(mean.total.rel + 1.96*standard_error(total.rel.growth),2))

ANOVA

############################
## Control Treatments ANOVA
############################

# Test for significant differences between the ambient and chamber controls
##############################################################################

# Subset data
controls_only = BudsSummary %>% filter(Treatment %in% c('Control', 'Pre_chamber_control', 'Post_chamber_control'))

# ANOVA
controls_anova = aov(EOS.DOY ~ Treatment, data = controls_only)

# summary
summary(controls_anova)
##              Df Sum Sq Mean Sq F value Pr(>F)
## Treatment     2     76   38.19   0.346  0.708
## Residuals   136  14992  110.24

Plot theme

# Plot theme
#####################################
  plotTheme1 = theme(
    legend.position   = "none",
    legend.background = element_rect(fill=NA, linewidth=0.5, linetype="solid"),
    legend.text       = element_text(color="black"),
    panel.background  = element_blank(),
    axis.text         = element_text(colour = "black"),
    panel.border      = element_rect(colour = "black", fill=NA),
    axis.line         = element_line(color = "black"),
    strip.background  = element_rect(fill=NA),
    strip.text        = element_text(colour = 'black'),
    plot.title        = element_text(face="bold",hjust = 0.5))

Plot

# Plot differences
##############################################################################

# relevel
controls_only$Treatment = fct_relevel(controls_only$Treatment, 'Control', 'Pre_chamber_control')

controls_boxplot = 
controls_only %>% ggplot(aes(Treatment, EOS.DOY, fill = 'grey')) +
  geom_boxplot(notch = T) +
  ylab("Bud set timing (DOY)") +
  plotTheme1 +
  theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 20), axis.text = element_text(size = 18))

controls_boxplot

Leaf senescence comparison

Data preparation

#####################
# Load leaf senescence data #
#####################


# import data
chlor.df = read.csv(paste(data.dir, '2022_phen_exp_spad.csv', sep = '/'))

chlor.df = chlor.df %>% 
  mutate(
    #date formatting
    measurement.date = as.Date(measurement.date, format = '%d.%m.%Y'),
    #Convert dates to DOY
    measurement.doy = day_of_year(measurement.date),
#merge controls
Treatment = ifelse(group %in% c("A","C"),"Control",
          ifelse(group %in% c("D"),"Pre_chamber_control",
                 ifelse(group %in% c("E"),"Pre_day", 
                          ifelse(group %in% c("F"),"Pre_night",
                             ifelse(group %in% c("G"),"Pre_full", 
                                   ifelse(group %in% c("H"),"Post_chamber_control",
                                        ifelse(group %in% c("I"),"Post_day",
                                              ifelse(group %in% c("J"),"Post_night",
                                                   ifelse(group %in% c("K"), "Post_full", "Other")))))))))) %>% 

# remove B treatment, and NAs
filter(!Treatment %in% c('Other'),
       !is.na(SPAD)) %>% 
  # group to individual
  group_by(id) %>% 
  # only keep trees with more than 8 measurements
  filter(n() >= 8) %>% 
  ungroup()

# factorise Treatment and relevel
chlor.df$Treatment = as.factor(chlor.df$Treatment)
chlor.df$Treatment = fct_relevel(chlor.df$Treatment, 'Control', 'Pre_chamber_control', 
                                 'Pre_night', 'Pre_full', 'Pre_day', 'Post_chamber_control',
                                 'Post_night', 'Post_full', 'Post_day')

str(chlor.df)
## tibble [1,980 × 7] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame)
##  $ id              : chr [1:1980] "D55" "D56" "D57" "D58" ...
##  $ number          : int [1:1980] 55 56 57 58 59 60 61 62 63 64 ...
##  $ group           : chr [1:1980] "D" "D" "D" "D" ...
##  $ measurement.date: Date[1:1980], format: "2022-06-28" "2022-06-28" ...
##  $ SPAD            : num [1:1980] 30.2 32 37.5 35.7 32.5 29.9 33.5 28.4 32 30.7 ...
##  $ measurement.doy : num [1:1980] 179 179 179 179 179 179 179 179 179 179 ...
##  $ Treatment       : Factor w/ 9 levels "Control","Pre_chamber_control",..: 2 2 2 2 2 2 2 2 2 2 ...
# Convert SPAD to chlorophyll content
SPADtoChl = function(SPAD){
  Chl = ifelse(SPAD==0, 0, -0.0029*(SPAD^2) + 1.175*SPAD + 3.8506)
  return(Chl)
}
#SPAD: unitless
#Chl: leaf chlorophyll in microgram / gram fresh weight
#Intercept forced to zero

#From Percival et al. 2008: The Potential of a Chlorophyll Content SPAD Meter to Quantify Nutrient Stress in Foliar Tissue of Sycamore (Acer pseudoplatanus), English Oak (Quercus robur), and European Beech (Fagus sylvatica)

chlor.df = chlor.df %>% 
  mutate(chlorophyll = SPADtoChl(SPAD))

Clean data

# Function to flag outliers
flag_iqr_outliers = function(x) {
  q1 = quantile(x, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
  q3 = quantile(x, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
  iqr = q3 - q1
  lower = q1 - 1.5 * iqr
  upper = q3 + 1.5 * iqr
  return(x < lower | x > upper)
}

# Apply the function per treatment and date
chlor_outliers_flagged = chlor.df %>%
  group_by(Treatment, measurement.date) %>%
  mutate(outlier = flag_iqr_outliers(chlorophyll)) %>%
  ungroup()

# visualise outliers

ggplot(chlor_outliers_flagged, aes(x = measurement.date, y = chlorophyll)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = outlier)) +
  geom_text(
    data = subset(chlor_outliers_flagged, outlier == TRUE),
    aes(label = id),
    vjust = -0.5,        # adjust vertical position
    size = 3,            # text size
    color = "black",     # override point color for visibility
    check_overlap = TRUE # avoid overlapping labels
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ Treatment) +
  labs(
    title = "Outliers by treatment and measurement date (IQR Method)") +
  theme_minimal()

##### Remove trees with > 1 outlier measurement and then delete outlier points

# Count outliers per tree
tree_outlier_counts = chlor_outliers_flagged %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  summarise(n_outliers = sum(outlier, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

# Identify trees with more than 1 outlier
trees_to_remove = tree_outlier_counts %>%
  filter(n_outliers > 1) %>%
  pull(id)

# Remove those trees from the dataset
chlor.df = chlor_outliers_flagged %>%
  filter(!(id %in% trees_to_remove) & !outlier) %>% 
  select(!outlier)

message("Removed trees with >1 outlier: ", paste(trees_to_remove, collapse = ", "))
## Removed trees with >1 outlier: A07, A09, C39, D66, D69, F107, F99, H143, I153, I156, I158, J174, J180, K182, K186, K190

Derive EOS

####################################################
## Relative chlorophyll levels
####################################################


chlor.df = chlor.df %>%
  group_by(id)%>%
  mutate(
    #get relative chlorophyll content per individual
    chlorophyll.rel = chlorophyll / max(chlorophyll),
    #save max chlorophyll content for each individual
    chlorophyll.max = chlorophyll.rel == max(chlorophyll.rel),
    #get date of maximum chlorophyll content per individual
    date.max.chl = measurement.date[which.max(chlorophyll)]) %>%
  ungroup()

######################################################################
## EOS (leaf senescence timing) derivation
######################################################################

# Interpolate EOS50 (date chlorophyll last dropped below 50%)
senescence.data.50 = chlor.df %>%
  group_by(id, Treatment) %>%
  # Find the last row with >50% chlorophyll in each group, keep only this row and next
  filter(row_number() <= max(which(chlorophyll.rel > 0.5))+1,
         row_number() >= max(which(chlorophyll.rel > 0.5))) %>%
  #Linear interpolation
  dplyr::summarize(EOS     = as.Date(approx(chlorophyll.rel, measurement.date, .5,  ties=min)$y, origin="1970-01-01")) %>%
  mutate(EOS.DOY    = yday(EOS),
         phenophase = "EOS50") %>%
  ungroup()
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'id'. You can override using the `.groups`
## argument.
# Mean EOS DOY per treatment
##############################

eos.doy.mean = 
  senescence.data.50 %>% 
  group_by(Treatment) %>% 
  summarise(meanEOS.DOY = round(mean(EOS.DOY),1),
            ci.lower.EOS = round(meanEOS.DOY - 1.96*standard_error(EOS.DOY),1),
            ci.upper.EOS = round(meanEOS.DOY + 1.96*standard_error(EOS.DOY),1))

# Observed trees per treatment after removal
table(senescence.data.50$Treatment, senescence.data.50$phenophase)
##                       
##                        EOS50
##   Control                 33
##   Pre_chamber_control     16
##   Pre_night               16
##   Pre_full                18
##   Pre_day                 18
##   Post_chamber_control    17
##   Post_night              16
##   Post_full               15
##   Post_day                15

ANOVA

############################
## Control Treatments ANOVA
############################

# Test for significant differences between the ambient and chamber controls
##############################################################################

# Subset data
sen.controls_only = senescence.data.50 %>% filter(Treatment %in% c('Control', 'Pre_chamber_control', 'Post_chamber_control'))

# ANOVA
sen.controls_anova = aov(EOS.DOY ~ Treatment, data = sen.controls_only)

# summary
summary(sen.controls_anova)
##             Df Sum Sq Mean Sq F value Pr(>F)
## Treatment    2   83.6   41.78   0.931  0.399
## Residuals   63 2826.2   44.86

Plot

# Plot differences
##############################################################################

# relevel
sen.controls_only$Treatment = fct_relevel(sen.controls_only$Treatment, 'Control', 'Pre_chamber_control')

sen.controls_boxplot = 
  sen.controls_only %>% ggplot(aes(Treatment, EOS.DOY, fill = 'grey')) +
  geom_boxplot(notch = T) +
  ylab("Bud set timing (DOY)") +
  plotTheme1 +
  theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 20), axis.text = element_text(size = 18))

sen.controls_boxplot
